rt2-1-1.title: Hands-on: HVL and inverse square at the boundary
rt2-1-1.intro: Put the lesson's two workhorse laws to work on an 80 Ci Ir-192 bay: walk the worker back to see dose rate fall with 1/d squared, then attenuate by fractions with half-value-layer slabs and budget exposure time like a Level II.

tasks.label: Double your distance and cut the rate fourfold
tasks.hint: Inverse square: 2x the distance means 1/4 the dose rate — no slabs needed.

tasks.label: Stack HVL slabs until the boundary reads under 2 mR/h
tasks.hint: Each slab halves the rate — count halvings like the lesson's halving table (about 3.3 HVLs per factor of ten).

tasks.label: Plan a 10+ minute job that stays under 20 mrem
tasks.hint: Dose = rate x time. If the planned dose runs hot, buy margin with distance or slabs before trimming time.

rt2-1-2.title: Hands-on: hit density, then beat source decay
rt2-1-2.intro: Work a real exposure calculation: choose a time that lands film density inside the 2.0-3.0 window, then jump 30 days ahead and re-expose — the Ir-192 has decayed, so scale time by t2 = t1 x (A1/A2) exactly like the lesson's big-three corrections.

tasks.label: Expose the film into the 2.0-3.0 density window
tasks.hint: Exposure is a product, E = A x t (reciprocity) — more time means more density.

tasks.label: Press '30 days later' and re-hit the window
tasks.hint: After 30 of 73.8 half-life days, activity is about 75% — multiply your old time by A1/A2 (about 1.33x).

rt2-1-3.title: Hands-on: sharpen definition, prove sensitivity
rt2-1-3.intro: Definition and sensitivity are the image-quality words you can rig for: the setup starts at Ug near 0.8 mm, so work the lesson's levers — film tight to the part, source backed off — to meet a 0.3 mm spec, then place the wire IQI where it proves the technique.

tasks.label: Drive Ug down to 0.3 mm or better
tasks.hint: Ug = F x OFD / SOD — close the film gap to shrink OFD AND lengthen SOD; one lever alone won't get you there.

tasks.label: Drag the wire IQI onto the source-side weld surface
tasks.hint: Sensitivity must be proven where image quality is worst — film-side placement is rejected.
